metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Address the first-patchset-on-new-branch 422 race: either (a) add AC prose and a run-log data point proving the branch replicates before the dispatch fires (e.g., by observing the Gerrit hook fires only after `replication-started`/`replication-done` events), or (b) document a mitigation (e.g., require branch creation to precede the first patchset push by a grace period, or have the operator pre-replicate the branch before first use) and add a corresponding AC.
findings.finding: The replication-lag race is not closed for the first patchset on a brand-new feature branch: when `feature/foo` is created in Gerrit and the developer immediately pushes patchset 1, g2p fires `patchset_created` and dispatches `workflow_dispatch(ref=refs/heads/feature/foo)` before the replication plugin has pushed `refs/heads/feature/foo` to GitHub (the branch did not exist there before). The new refspec makes subsequent patchsets work, but the very first patchset on a new branch recreates the exact 422/no-op failure the story exists to fix. Unlike the `refs/changes/*` lag (which checkout-gerrit-change-action tolerates via a Gerrit-direct fallback), the `workflow_dispatch` ref itself has no fallback — a 422 from the GitHub API means the CI dispatch is silently dropped and Verified is never cast. The plan's AC2 requires proving that 'a newly-created feature branch auto-replicates' but does not require proof that it does so before the first patchset's dispatch fires, leaving this ordering hazard undetected by the ACs.
findings.location: What/FIX section + AC2 ('replication.config replicates feature/* ... a newly-created feature branch auto-replicates to the GitHub mirror (proven)')
findings.suggested_fix: In the What/FIX section, add: 'NOTE: a brand-new feature branch may not yet exist on the GitHub mirror when patchset 1 fires; operators should push the branch ref to the GitHub mirror (or wait for replication confirmation) before the first patchset is submitted for review, OR verify via run-log that the replication plugin pushes refs/heads/feature/foo synchronously before the hooks plugin fires patchset_created.' Then add an AC variant: 'Run-log evidence: patchset 1 on a freshly-created feature branch receives Verified (not just patchset 2+).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (and document in the replication tradeoff note) that the GitHub mirror repo has no push-triggered workflows or branch-protection rules that would fire on newly replicated feature/* refs, or explicitly call out the side effect and its mitigation.
findings.finding: The plan adds feature/* push replication to the GitHub mirror repo but does not acknowledge whether the mirror repo has any GitHub Actions workflows with push: branches: [feature/**] (or wildcard) triggers that would now fire on every replicated feature/* ref push, creating unintended CI runs or notification noise on the mirror side.
findings.location: What (REVISED) / FIX — replication.config change; also AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line note to the FIX section: 'Verified: github mirror repo has no push: feature/* workflow triggers or branch-protection rules that would activate on replicated refs.' If such triggers exist, scope their disablement or exemption as part of this story.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge (or address) that adding `refs/heads/feature/*` to the GitHub mirror will trigger push-based runs in `test.yml`, `eval.yml`, and `optionality.yml` (all use `branches: ["**"]`), and document whether this is acceptable, requires branch filters, or is intentionally tolerated.
findings.finding: Three GitHub Actions workflows — test.yml, eval.yml, optionality.yml — all trigger on push: branches: ["**"], meaning every feature/* push replicated to the GitHub mirror will fan out these CI runs. This is an unenumerated side-effect of the replication.config change that the plan does not acknowledge.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a concrete delete mechanism for feature/* refs on the GitHub mirror — either a `push = :refs/heads/feature/*:refs/heads/feature/*` delete-refspec triggered on merge-back, a documented deploy-key `git push origin --delete` step in the merge-back recipe, or an explicit note that the S6 inventory script performs this deletion and prove it in the AC2 run-log evidence.
findings.finding: The plan makes refs/heads/feature/* transiently public on the GitHub mirror but specifies no replication-level or deploy-key-level delete mechanism. The only specified cleanup is a vague 'teardown handled by S6 inventory + merge-back cleanup.' If the S6 manual step is skipped or delayed, unmerged feature-branch code remains permanently visible on the public mirror with no automatic expiry or deletion. The replication.config uses NON-mirror, NON-force pushes with no delete-ref refspec, so the replication plugin will never remove a feature/* branch from GitHub on its own.
findings.location: FIX section (replication.config change) / AC2 ('feature branch auto-replicates to the GitHub mirror (proven)')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a documented operator step in the merge-back recipe (and in the S6 inventory script) that runs `git push github --delete refs/heads/feature/<name>` (using the existing deploy key) immediately after the merge change submits and replication advances main. Optionally, document that `replication flush --url git@github.com:navapbc/rebar.git` after the Gerrit branch deletion is insufficient (the plugin only pushes, never deletes, without mirror=true) so the explicit push --delete step is required.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the back-out procedure to require a paired `git revert` (or a PR removing the `push = refs/heads/feature/*:refs/heads/feature/*` line from `infra/gerrit/replication.config`) so the committed file and the live config remain in sync; document that the `.bak-s3` on-box snapshot is a safety net only, not the authoritative back-out artifact.
findings.finding: The plan's back-out for the replication.config change ('remove the line; a .bak-s3 of the prior config is kept on the box') is defined only as a live-file operation, with no mention of reverting the committed infra/gerrit/replication.config. This creates a drift state: after back-out, the live /etc/replication.config no longer contains the feature/* line, but the repo still does — the next execution of setup-replication.sh (which copies the repo file to the live path) would silently re-add the line, undoing the back-out without operator awareness.
findings.location: What section — FIX paragraph: 'Back-out: remove the line; a .bak-s3 of the prior config is kept on the box'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Back-out note: 'Back-out is a two-step operation: (1) remove or comment-out the `push = refs/heads/feature/*` line from the committed `infra/gerrit/replication.config` and merge that change; (2) run `setup-replication.sh` (or manually copy the reverted file) so the live config matches. The `.bak-s3` snapshot on the box is a break-glass fallback only — relying on it alone leaves the repo file diverged from the live state.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the rollback plan to specify how already-replicated feature/* branches on the GitHub mirror are removed when the replication.config line is rolled back (e.g. a one-liner `git push github --delete refs/heads/feature/*` or equivalent, not deferred to S6).
findings.finding: The rollback plan ('remove the line') stops future feature/* replication but does not address already-replicated feature/* branches that persist on the GitHub mirror after a rollback — leaving the mirror in a dirty state the plan attributes to a future story (S6).
findings.location: What (FIX paragraph): 'Back-out: remove the line; a .bak-s3 of the prior config is kept on the box' and 'teardown handled by S6 inventory + merge-back cleanup'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the back-out description: 'On rollback, prune mirror refs with: ssh gerrit gerrit replication start --all or run `git push github --delete refs/heads/feature/BRANCHNAME` for each replicated branch (list from S3 run log). S6 handles ongoing cleanup; rollback requires immediate pruning of branches created during S3.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a self-contained or minimal RETIRE path for `refs/heads/feature/*` refs on the GitHub mirror within this story — e.g., a merge-back cleanup step, a `git push origin --delete refs/heads/feature/X` hook triggered on Gerrit branch deletion, or at minimum a runbook entry that does not depend solely on S6 completing.
findings.finding: The RETIRE path for `refs/heads/feature/*` refs on the GitHub mirror is deferred entirely to S6 (a future story) with no self-contained cleanup fallback in this story. If S6 is not executed (deprioritized, delayed, or the epic is abandoned), feature branch refs accumulate on the GitHub mirror indefinitely, and there is no defined owner or trigger to clean them up.
findings.location: What (REVISED) — FIX section: 'deleted after merge-back; teardown handled by S6 inventory + merge-back cleanup'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Gerrit `ref-updated` hook (or replication plugin `remote.github.remoteNameStyle` + `deleteProjectName` equivalent) that pushes a delete of the remote feature/* ref when the local branch is deleted in Gerrit after merge-back. This makes the lifecycle self-contained within S3 and removes the S6 dependency for cleanup.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a durable doc artifact — at minimum a comment block in `infra/gerrit/replication.config` AND an entry in CLAUDE.md or a short ADR — explaining that feature/* must be mirrored to GitHub for g2p workflow_dispatch to resolve, the transient-visibility tradeoff, and that S6 owns teardown; cross-reference from the ini/dispatch spec note required by AC1.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a non-obvious, cross-system architectural contract — feature/* branches must be replicated to the GitHub mirror for g2p workflow_dispatch to resolve, with transient public visibility and teardown ownership delegated to S6 — but names no durable doc artifact (ADR, CLAUDE.md entry, or design-doc section) where this decision and its tradeoffs are recorded. The inline 'Documented tradeoff' note in the What section is ephemeral to the story and won't be discoverable by future maintainers of replication.config or gerrit-verify.yaml.
findings.location: What section — 'Documented tradeoff: feature branches become transiently visible...' / AC: replication.config AC
findings.suggested_fix:
In `infra/gerrit/replication.config`, add a multi-line comment above the feature/* push line, e.g.:
# REQUIRED FOR CI: g2p dispatches workflow_dispatch(ref=refs/heads/{GERRIT_BRANCH});
# the ref must exist on the GitHub mirror or the dispatch is a 422 no-op.
# feature/* is transiently visible; S6 (inventory + merge-back) handles deletion.
# See docs/adr/003-gerrit-feature-branch-replication.md for full context.
Also create docs/adr/003-gerrit-feature-branch-replication.md covering the decision, tradeoffs, and teardown ownership.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'behaves sanely' in AC6 with a specific observable assertion, e.g.: 'when two patchsets are uploaded concurrently, the older gerrit-verify.yaml run is cancelled (shown by a GitHub Actions cancelled status) AND the newer run completes and casts Verified on the correct patchset — evidenced by a linked run log.'
findings.finding: AC6 requires that `concurrency: cancel-in-progress` 'behaves sanely across concurrent branch + merge changes' but 'sanely' is a subjective, undefined outcome. No specific observable assertion is given (e.g., 'older run is cancelled within N seconds', 'the newer run produces a Verified vote', 'no duplicate Verified votes are cast'), so no proving command can confirm the criterion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC6; What section fourth bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC6 as: 'Run-log evidence: when a feature/* patchset is uploaded while a prior run for the same branch is in-flight, the prior run shows status=cancelled in GitHub Actions and the new run completes and casts Verified on the correct change; and a merge change overlapping a branch run does not cancel the merge run (separate concurrency group).'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the exact file path (e.g. `docs/g2p-dispatch-note.md` or an annotated block in `infra/gerrit/gerrit_to_platform.ini`) where the AC1 verified statement and source citation must be committed, so the artifact location is unambiguous.
findings.finding: AC1 references 'the ini spec / a short g2p-dispatch note' as the destination artifact but never names the file path or section where the verified statement should be committed. An executing agent cannot determine where to write this artifact without guessing, blocking the AC.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1; What section first bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to AC1: 'Record in `docs/adr/g2p-branch-dispatch.md` (new file, committed to main), citing lfit/releng-gerrit_to_platform@44d5d46.' This pins the artifact and makes the AC checkable by `git log --all -- docs/adr/g2p-branch-dispatch.md`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or merge into the replication.config AC) that verifies the .bak-s3 backup of replication.config exists on the Gerrit host before the live deployment is marked done.
findings.finding: The back-out procedure ('.bak-s3 of the prior config is kept on the box') is described as a concrete deliverable in the What/FIX section but has no covering AC, so it cannot be verified at story close.
findings.location: What (FIX paragraph, back-out sentence) vs Acceptance Criteria (all six ACs)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC2: '...AND a .bak-s3 snapshot of the pre-change replication.config is present on the Gerrit host (proven by ls output in the run log).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name `infra/gerrit/setup-replication.sh` (or the equivalent manual `cp` + chown) as the explicit deployment step that copies the updated `replication.config` to the Gerrit site before `autoReload` can pick it up.
findings.finding: The plan says 'Deploy to the running Gerrit via the gerrit_etc volume; autoReload = true' but never names setup-replication.sh, which is the actual operator tool that copies infra/gerrit/replication.config to the Gerrit site etc dir. Without invoking this script the edited repo file never reaches the live site for autoReload to pick up.
findings.location: What / FIX section: 'Deploy to the running Gerrit via the gerrit_etc volume; autoReload = true'
findings.suggested_fix: In the FIX section and AC2, replace 'Deploy to the running Gerrit via the gerrit_etc volume' with 'Run infra/gerrit/setup-replication.sh (copies infra/gerrit/replication.config to /var/gerrit/site/etc/replication.config); autoReload = true then reloads the plugin with no Gerrit restart required.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Note that `materialize-g2p-config.sh` (called by `compose-up.sh`) holds a separate copy of `replication.config` for g2p owner/repo discovery, and that this copy only refreshes on the next compose-up, not on `autoReload`.
findings.finding: materialize-g2p-config.sh (called by compose-up.sh) copies replication.config into the g2p bind-mount at /var/gerrit/site/g2p-config/replication.config. This copy is only refreshed at compose-up time, not on autoReload. The plan's 'Deploy to the running Gerrit via the gerrit_etc volume; autoReload = true' framing does not enumerate this consumer, so if the live replication.config is hot-edited and autoReloaded but compose-up is not re-run, the g2p copy remains stale.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the replication-lag race between `patchset_created` dispatch and GitHub mirror availability is handled — e.g., g2p retry-with-backoff on 422, a replication-completion hook, or a documented acceptable-lag SLO with evidence it is met under load.
findings.finding: Post-fix, a replication-lag race can recreate the exact 422 failure: g2p fires `workflow_dispatch` on `patchset_created` before the replication plugin has finished pushing the new `refs/heads/feature/*` ref to the GitHub mirror. The plan adds no retry/backoff on the dispatch side and no ordering guarantee between replication completion and dispatch, so the root-cause failure (ref not found → 422 → no Verified) remains possible under load or network delay.
findings.location: What (REVISED) — FIX section / AC: 'Run-log evidence: feature/* patchset triggers gerrit-verify.yaml and casts Verified (post-fix)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the unverifiable `get_change_refspec` assertion with a direct citation: read the relevant source in `lfit/releng-gerrit_to_platform@44d5d46` (e.g., the hook script that sets `GERRIT_REFSPEC`) or the `checkout-gerrit-change-action` source, confirm the refspec shape for merge commits is `refs/changes/NN/NNNN/P`, and record the file+line citation in the plan.
findings.finding: The plan asserts '`get_change_refspec` computes `refs/changes/NN/NNNN/P` identically for merge + normal commits — the patchset ref IS the merge tree' as a named-function behavioral fact, but `get_change_refspec` returns zero matches anywhere in this repository. The function name appears in neither the in-repo g2p integration code nor the `checkout-gerrit-change-action` usage in `gerrit-verify.yaml`. This is an unverifiable codebase assertion: the claim may refer to external g2p source, but no grounding evidence exists in the local tree, and the plan's 'run-log evidence' qualifier does not substitute for source verification.
findings.location: What section — 'Verify `checkout-gerrit-change-action` fetches a MERGE refspec so CI builds the actual merge tree (get_change_refspec computes refs/changes/NN/NNNN/P identically for merge + normal commits — the patchset ref IS the merge tree; run-log evidence)'
findings.suggested_fix: Read `https://github.com/lfit/releng-gerrit_to_platform/blob/44d5d46/gerrit_to_platform/patchset_created.py` (or equivalent) to locate where `GERRIT_REFSPEC` is constructed; if the value is simply the Gerrit-provided `$GERRIT_REFSPEC` env var passed through by the hooks plugin unchanged, document that the hooks plugin always supplies the patchset ref as `refs/changes/NN/NNNN/P` regardless of merge vs. normal commit, and cite the Gerrit hooks plugin documentation as the source rather than a non-existent local function.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observability requirement: specify how replication failures and g2p dispatch errors will be surfaced (e.g., Gerrit replication plugin error logs shipped to a dashboard, a periodic synthetic test that creates a feature/* ref and checks for a Verified vote, or an alert on missing Verified within N minutes of patchset_created).
findings.finding: No observability is specified for the new failure points introduced: replication plugin errors (push failures, 422s from GitHub) and g2p dispatch errors are not instrumented with a metric, log alert, or dashboard. Operators have no way to detect that feature/* CI is silently broken again post-deploy.
findings.location: What (REVISED) — FIX section; Acceptance Criteria (all ACs are one-time run-log evidence, not ongoing monitoring)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the contradiction between the plan's 'no Gerrit restart (verified)' claim and `setup-replication.sh`'s documented restart-required path (ALLOW_REMOTE_RELOAD=0, remote plugin admin disabled): either confirm that a direct volume-edit of `replication.config` triggers autoReload independently of the deployment script, or update the plan's deployment procedure to acknowledge a restart is needed (and confirm the maintenance window / impact).
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'autoReload = true in replication.config means the plugin re-reads on change with NO Gerrit restart (verified)' as a deployment capability, but the actual deployment tool (infra/gerrit/setup-replication.sh) explicitly defaults to the restart path: ALLOW_REMOTE_RELOAD=0 and the comment states 'REMOTE PLUGIN ADMIN IS DISABLED on this instance, so the new replication.config is loaded at Gerrit STARTUP. RESTART Gerrit to load it.' The plan's 'no restart' claim is contradicted by the codebase's own description of the live instance configuration, leaving the no-restart deployment path unverified.
findings.location: What (REVISED) — FIX section: 'Deploy to the running Gerrit via the gerrit_etc volume; autoReload = true in replication.config means the plugin re-reads on change with NO Gerrit restart (verified)' / infra/gerrit/setup-replication.sh lines 14–18, 103–110
findings.suggested_fix: In the FIX section, replace 'autoReload = true ... NO Gerrit restart (verified)' with one of: (a) 'Direct edit to the gerrit_etc volume file triggers autoReload (no restart) — verified by observing the replication log for the new refspec within N seconds of the edit'; or (b) 'A brief Gerrit restart is required to load the new refspec (consistent with setup-replication.sh); confirm the maintenance window is acceptable.' Also add a note to setup-replication.sh's restart guidance clarifying that autoReload covers incremental edits to an already-loaded config, distinguishing first-load vs incremental-change cases.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the 'no restart' autoReload claim with setup-replication.sh's documented behavior for this instance (ALLOW_REMOTE_RELOAD=0): either confirm that copying the file to the live site IS sufficient for autoReload to pick it up without a restart (and cite evidence), or update AC2 to acknowledge a brief Gerrit restart is required and plan for a maintenance window.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'autoReload = true in replication.config means the plugin re-reads on change with NO Gerrit restart (verified)' as the deployment mechanism for the replication.config change, but the actual deployment tool (infra/gerrit/setup-replication.sh) explicitly contradicts this for the live instance: 'REMOTE PLUGIN ADMIN IS DISABLED on this instance, so the new replication.config is loaded at Gerrit STARTUP. RESTART Gerrit to load it.' (lines 14-18). The ALLOW_REMOTE_RELOAD default is 0. autoReload handles subsequent in-place edits to an ALREADY-LOADED config file, but since the replication plugin reads the file on startup under this instance's configuration, adding a new push refspec requires either (a) running setup-replication.sh AND restarting Gerrit, or (b) enabling remote plugin admin (ALLOW_REMOTE_RELOAD=1). The 'no restart' claim is asserted as verified but is contradicted by the codebase's own description of the live setup.
findings.location: What (REVISED) — FIX section: 'Deploy to the running Gerrit via the gerrit_etc volume; autoReload = true in replication.config means the plugin re-reads on change with NO Gerrit restart (verified)' / infra/gerrit/setup-replication.sh lines 14–18, 57, 103–110
findings.suggested_fix: In the FIX section, replace 'autoReload = true in replication.config means the plugin re-reads on change with NO Gerrit restart (verified)' with 'Run setup-replication.sh to copy the updated replication.config to the Gerrit site; autoReload = true re-reads the file on disk change (no explicit `gerrit plugin reload` command needed), but confirm against the live instance whether a Gerrit restart is required given ALLOW_REMOTE_RELOAD=0.' Also update AC2 to reflect the actual deployment procedure.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
